title: US-Iran direct talks begin in Pakistan amid disputes, distrust
summary_full: Direct US-Iran talks commenced in Pakistan Saturday, following a fragile ceasefire.  US Vice President JD Vance met with Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf to discuss advancing the peace, amidst ongoing Israeli attacks.  Regional powers, including China, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar, are present to facilitate discussions on critical issues like compensation for war damages.
